name: Mechanism predicting the PMNS lepton mixing pattern from structural inputs
closability_note: Modular flavor at levels 3-5 (A_4, S_4, A_5) is the active program; multiple published models reproduce all 3 angles + CP phase but uniqueness is unproven.
summary: The PMNS angles and CP phase have a specific structure (near-trimaximal atmospheric, large solar, small but nonzero reactor, CP phase ~ -90 deg) that no framework derives.
what_closes_it: A mechanism predicting all 3 PMNS angles and the Dirac CP phase from a finite modular group + weight assignments + representation choice, with no remaining free parameters beyond an overall mass scale. Bonus if it also fixes the Majorana phases and absolute mass scale.
scale_hint: Seesaw scale (10^9 - 10^14 GeV) for the right-handed-neutrino sector; modular vacuum value tau set at the GUT or compactification scale.

principles_at_stake.note: PMNS mixing breaks lepton-flavor conservation in the active-neutrino sector. The closing mechanism must reproduce the observed mixing while respecting the charged-lepton-sector LFU constraints (mu -> e gamma, etc.).

literature_anchors.citation: Kobayashi, Tanaka, Tatsuishi, 'Neutrino mixing from finite modular groups', PRD 98, 016004 (2018)
Penedo and Petcov, 'Lepton masses and mixing from modular S_4 symmetry', Nucl. Phys. B 939, 292 (2019)
Ding and King, 'Neutrino mass and mixing with modular symmetry', Rept. Prog. Phys. 87, 084201 (2024)
